description: allocate_port (bin/devenv:167-172) calls bare python3 which is not installed in the devenv container. Function always returns 1, causing SSH port allocation to silently fail. Rewrite to use 'uv run python3 -c ...' per project convention. See research/testing-standard/research.md Open Decision #6.
title: Fix allocate_port: rewrite to use uv run python3

description: Create tests/bats/log_primitives.bats with tests for shared/bash/log.sh. Test scenarios: log_debug visible at DEBUG level, suppressed at WARNING; log_info visible at INFO, suppressed at WARNING; log_warning visible at WARNING/INFO, suppressed at ERROR; log_error always visible; die exits 1 with ERROR format; output format matches [timestamp] [LEVEL] pattern (no timestamp assertions). Key constraint: DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL is readonly — each test needing a different level must run in a fresh subshell that sets DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL before sourcing log.sh. See research/testing-standard/research.md for full scenario table.
title: Write log_primitives.bats — log level filtering and die tests

description: Create tests/bats/build_devenv_cli.bats. Prerequisites: fake docker handles 'docker info'. Scenarios: no args (exit 1, Usage:), --stage no value (exit 1), --stage invalid (exit 1), --stage base (exit 0, docker build with correct Dockerfile), --tool jq (exit 0), --tool nonexistent (exit 1), --tool tree-sitter (dependency chain — fake docker images must return tools-node:latest), --project /nonexistent (exit 1), --unknown (exit 1). Also includes independent unit tests for duplicated primitives: resolve_project_path (line 64) and derive_project_image_suffix (line 85) from bin/build-devenv, tested separately from bin/devenv copies per spec section 4. See research/testing-standard/research.md.
title: Write build_devenv_cli.bats — argument parsing and docker intent tests

description: Create tests/bats/devenv_primitives.bats. Source bin/devenv (BASH_SOURCE guard prevents main execution). Test 10 primitives: resolve_project_path (., absolute, relative, nonexistent), resolve_container_project_path (under HOME, outside HOME triggers die in subshell), derive_container_name (standard path, special chars, empty), derive_project_label (parent/basename), derive_project_image_suffix (sanitized, special chars, empty), is_valid_port (1/22/65535 valid; 0/65536/abc/empty/negative invalid), allocate_port (assert exit 1 until python3 fix lands), get_image_name (with/without .devenv/Dockerfile), build_mounts (mandatory mounts, SSH_AUTH_SOCK conditional, config file conditionals via temp HOME), build_env_vars (TERM, SSH_AUTH_SOCK conditional). See research/testing-standard/research.md Function/Block-Level Detail.
title: Write devenv_primitives.bats — unit tests for 10 primitives

description: Create the full test directory tree per spec: tests/bats/, tests/e2e-human/, tests/helpers/, tests/fixtures/bin/. Create tests/helpers/test_env.bash (setup/teardown with HOME/PATH/LC_ALL isolation, DEVENV_HOME export) and tests/helpers/assert_output.bash (assert_exit_code, assert_stdout_contains, assert_stderr_contains, assert_stdout_empty, assert_stderr_empty, refute_stdout_contains). See research/testing-standard/research.md File-Level Change Map.
title: Create test directory structure and helpers

description: Create tests/bats/install_devenv_cli.bats. Prerequisites: DEVENV_HOME must point to directory with bin/build-devenv and bin/devenv (or create stubs). Scenarios: --help (exit 0, Usage:), default install (creates symlinks in temp HOME/.local/bin/, exit 0, correct targets), idempotent install (run twice succeeds), --uninstall (removes symlinks, exit 0), --uninstall already uninstalled (exit 0, INFO message), --source /custom/path (custom symlink targets), --source no value (exit 1), --unknown (exit 1). See research/testing-standard/research.md.
title: Write install_devenv_cli.bats — install/uninstall contract tests

description: bd doctor's Git Hooks check reports hooks as @unknown/0.0.0 and incompatible with Dolt backend, even immediately after running bd hooks install --force. This is a known upstream bug (steveyegge/beads#2244) fixed in commit b96483fc. The fix teaches parseBDHookVersion() and CheckGitHooksDoltCompatibility() to recognize the section-marker hook format. Resolution: upgrade bd past commit b96483fc.
title: bd doctor reports hook incompatibility even after bd hooks install --force

description: specs/testing-standard.md line 138 references LOG_LEVEL but the implementation in shared/bash/log.sh:15 uses DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL. The code is correct (namespaced to avoid collisions). Update the spec to match the implementation. See research/testing-standard/research.md Open Decision #5.
title: Fix spec bug: LOG_LEVEL should be DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL in testing-standard.md

description: bats-core is not installed in the devenv container. Add it to the devenv Dockerfile (via shared/tools/ or docker/devenv/Dockerfile.devenv) so it's always available. This aligns with the project pattern for tooling. See research/testing-standard/research.md Open Decision #1. This is a prerequisite for all test files.
title: Install bats-core in devenv image

description: Epic: Implement the testing standard defined in specs/testing-standard.md. The codebase currently has zero test coverage, no test infrastructure (bats-core, helpers, fixtures), and two bugs identified during gap analysis (allocate_port broken, spec references wrong variable name). This epic covers: installing bats-core, creating test helpers and fixtures, writing unit tests for all primitives, CLI contract tests for all scripts, e2e placeholders, shellcheck integration, and fixing the two identified bugs. See research/testing-standard/research.md for full gap analysis with 26 gaps identified (2 critical, 3 high, 4 medium, 2 low).
title: Implement Testing Standard

description: Create tests/fixtures/bin/docker — executable bash script that: logs all invocations to DOCKER_LOG or BATS_TMPDIR/docker_calls.log, returns exit 0 by default, supports FAKE_DOCKER_EXIT for exit code override, supports FAKE_DOCKER_OUTPUT for canned stdout, handles subcommands: ps, stop, run, exec, build, info, images, volume, system. Start minimal and add subcommand-specific handling as tests require it. See research/testing-standard/research.md Open Decision #4.
title: Create fake docker fixture

description: Create tests/bats/devenv_cli.bats. CLI contract tests running bin/devenv as executable with fake docker on PATH. Prerequisites: fake docker handles 'docker info' for validate_docker. Scenarios: devenv list (exit 0, NAME/SSH/STATUS/STARTED columns), devenv stop --all with no containers (exit 0, INFO message), devenv stop no target (exit 1, error), devenv volume rm no args (exit 1), devenv volume rm --all with in-use volume (exit 1), devenv volume no subcommand (exit 1), devenv volume list (exit 0), devenv volume rm --force (exit 0), devenv volume rm with y confirmation, devenv help (exit 0, Usage:). Use regex patterns for die output: .*[ERROR.*].*<message>.*. See research/testing-standard/research.md.
title: Write devenv_cli.bats — CLI contract tests for devenv

description: Create tests/e2e-human/README.md documenting the opt-in Docker E2E testing process and tests/e2e-human/devenv_e2e.bats as a minimal placeholder. These are low-priority items that document how to run integration tests that require real Docker. See research/testing-standard/research.md gaps #11 and #12.
title: Create e2e-human test placeholders

description: Final verification task: run the complete bats/ test suite and confirm no real docker calls are made. All tests must use the fake docker fixture. This validates gap #16 (default test run requires no Docker) and serves as the acceptance criteria for the epic. Run shellcheck on all scripts as well. See research/testing-standard/research.md Suggested Implementation Order steps 12-13.
title: Verify full test suite passes without Docker

description: resolve_project_path exists in both bin/devenv (line 87) and bin/build-devenv (line 64). derive_project_image_suffix exists in both bin/devenv (line 150) and bin/build-devenv (line 85). The spec says to test each copy independently 'until the duplication is resolved by extracting a shared library.' This task is to extract the duplicated primitives to a shared bash library (e.g., shared/bash/paths.sh). Lower priority — tests should be written first per spec guidance. See research/testing-standard/research.md Open Decision #3.
title: Extract shared primitives to common library

description: shellcheck v0.11.0 is installed but there's no runner script or CI integration. Create a way to run shellcheck on all scripts (bin/devenv, bin/build-devenv, scripts/install-devenv, shared/bash/log.sh) and verify zero warnings. This could be a simple shell script or documented run command. See research/testing-standard/research.md gap #17.
title: Create shellcheck runner and verify all scripts pass
